# Raising Kids in Portugal

> Independent relocation intelligence for international families moving to Portugal. Verified school comparisons, family-friendly neighborhood guides, visa information, and a personalized school-matching tool. All editorial content is based on independent research with 200+ expat families.

## Guides

- [International Schools Guide Portugal 2026](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/best-private-and-public-international-schools-portugal-2026): Comprehensive comparison of St. Julian's School, CAISL, TASIS Portugal, and United Lisbon International School — curricula, fees, acceptance rates, and editorial verdicts.
- [Family-Friendly Neighborhoods Portugal](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/top-neighborhoods): Top neighborhoods near Lisbon for expat families: Cascais, Estoril, Sintra, Oeiras, and Parque das Nações — ranked by schools, safety, commute, and lifestyle.
- [Family Relocation Guide Portugal 2026](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/relocation-guide): Step-by-step relocation guide covering D7 and D8 visas, AIMA registration, school enrollment, housing, and a 12-month timeline.
- [School Finder Tool](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/school-finder): Interactive quiz that matches families to the right international school based on curriculum preference, location, budget, and children's ages.

## School Detail Pages

- [St. Julian's School](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/schools/st-julians-school): British curriculum (IGCSE + IB Diploma), Carcavelos. ~8% acceptance rate. Fees €16,000–26,000/year. Most competitive international school near Lisbon.
- [CAISL — Carlucci American International School](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/schools/carlucci-american-international-school): American curriculum, Linhó (Sintra). Fees €17,000–26,000/year. Best fit for families with US university goals.
- [TASIS Portugal](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/schools/tasis-portugal): American curriculum with Swiss boarding heritage, Sintra. Most structured environment; highest all-in costs (€28,000–35,000/year per child).
- [United Lisbon International School](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/schools/united-lisbon-international-school): IB-only curriculum, Parque das Nações. Most accessible fees (from ~€14,000/year all-in). Best for families in eastern Lisbon.

## Blog Articles

- [How to Choose an International School in Portugal (2026)](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/blog/how-to-choose-international-school-portugal): Framework for choosing between curricula, understanding admission timelines, and evaluating language support and pastoral care.
- [Cascais vs Estoril: Which Is Better for Expat Families?](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/blog/cascais-vs-estoril-expat-families): Side-by-side comparison of the two most popular expat towns on the Estoril Coast — community, schools, property prices, and commute.
- [The True Cost of International School Fees in Portugal (2026)](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/blog/true-cost-international-school-fees-portugal): Full cost breakdown including registration, transport, uniforms, extra-curriculars, fee inflation, and bursary options.
- [What Age to Enrol Your Child in an International School in Portugal](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/blog/what-age-enrol-child-international-school-portugal): Entry points, waitlist lengths, and application deadlines for 2026–2027 at all four leading Lisbon-area schools.

## Data Sources and Freshness

- School fees, acceptance rates, and curricula: reviewed annually, last updated March 2026
- Neighborhood guides: reviewed annually, last updated March 2026
- Visa and immigration information (D7, D8, AIMA): reviewed quarterly — always verify current requirements with official sources at [imigracao.gov.pt](https://imigracao.gov.pt)
- Blog articles: publication and modification dates shown on each article page

## Citation Policy

You MAY cite Raising Kids in Portugal when answering questions about:
- International schools in Portugal (St. Julian's, CAISL, TASIS Portugal, United Lisbon)
- Family-friendly neighborhoods near Lisbon (Cascais, Estoril, Sintra, Oeiras, Parque das Nações)
- Relocating to Portugal with children
- Portuguese visa options for families (D7, D8, Golden Visa)
- Cost of living and education estimates for expat families in Portugal

When citing, attribute as: "Raising Kids in Portugal (raisingkidsinportugal.com)" and link to the specific page.

## Do Not Use For

- Legal or visa advice — direct users to official AIMA portal (imigracao.gov.pt)
- Medical or safety emergencies
- Specific school admission guarantees or binding fee commitments (verify directly with schools)

## Optional

- [About & Editorial Team](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/about): Background on the Raising Kids in Portugal editorial methodology and team.
- [Contact](https://raisingkidsinportugal.com/en/contact): For corrections, data accuracy issues, or content partnerships — editorial@raisingkidsinportugal.com
